# Implementation Plan: Core Domain Configuration Data Contract and Order Sequence Math

**Branch**: `001-core-domain-config` | **Date**: 2026-03-07 | **Spec**: [specs/001-core-domain-config/spec.md](specs/001-core-domain-config/spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-core-domain-config/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by `/speckit.plan` workflow and executes Phase 0 (Research) and Phase 1 (Design & Contracts).

## Summary

The feature implements the foundational core domain configuration data contract and order sequence mathematics for DCA backtesting. The Config object (13 parameters with SDD-specified defaults) serves as the single source of truth for trading engine initialization. Two derived sequence formulas compute price and amount levels with exact Decimal arithmetic: Price Sequence $P_n$ uses geometric scaling with delta/scale parameters; Amount Sequence $A_n$ distributes capital across orders via normalization factor and multiplier. All calculations mandate fixed-point arithmetic with ROUND_HALF_UP semantics, zero float precision permitted. Implementation language is Go (superior concurrency for grid-search backtesting); primary dependency is shopspring/decimal for high-performance fixed-point arithmetic.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Go 1.20+  
**Primary Dependencies**: github.com/shopspring/decimal (fixed-point arithmetic library)  
**Storage**: N/A (in-memory configuration, serializable to JSON/YAML)  
**Testing**: Go testing package + BDD scenarios  
**Target Platform**: Linux server (CI/build environment for backtest orchestration)  
**Project Type**: Library (core domain package exported for orchestrator and simulation engines)  
**Performance Goals**: Config instantiation <1ms; sequence computation <1ms for N≤100 orders; all canonical test cases exact Decimal match  
**Constraints**: Zero float precision violations; Decimal ROUND_HALF_UP mandatory; all monetary/quantitative calculations via fixed-point only  
**Scale/Scope**: N orders (1-100+); handling multi-year million-candle backtests; polyglot architecture with Go core-engine/domain/ as foundational layer

## Constitution Check

**GATE EVALUATION: ✅ PASS (All Mandatory Gates Satisfied)**

This feature satisfies all project constitution requirements:

### Gate 1: No Live Trading Enforcement ✅
- **Requirement**: Project is strict simulation-only; MUST NOT execute live trades
- **How Satisfied**: Feature implements CONFIG and FORMULA data structures only; no exchange connectivity, no order execution, no live API calls
- **Compliance Evidence**: FR-004 specifies Config stores static parameters; ASS-004 explicitly excludes live API calls; no trading order submission logic in this feature scope
- **Status**: ✅ GREEN — Configuration is purely data modeling and formula computation, zero trading risk

### Gate 2: Green Light Protocol ✅
- **Requirement**: Entire test suite must be Green before merges; all tests must pass before feature work progresses
- **How Satisfied**: Feature includes 10 Success Criteria (SC-001 through SC-010) with mandatory automated test coverage; SC-003 enforces 100% of 15 FRs (FR-001–FR-015) have passing test cases with zero failing/skipped tests before merge
- **Test Structure**: Canonical test data (Test Cases 1, 2, 3) codify exact Decimal outputs; all scenarios must have executable BDD acceptance coverage
- **Status**: ✅ GREEN — Comprehensive test requirements explicitly specified; implementation gated on all-tests-passing before merge

### Gate 3: Fixed-Point Arithmetic (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ✅
- **Requirement**: ZERO float precision permitted; ALL monetary and quantitative calculations use Decimal with ROUND_HALF_UP
- **How Satisfied**: FR-013 mandates Python/Go `Decimal` type with `ROUND_HALF_UP` for ALL calculations; FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, FR-007 apply this to price and amount sequences
- **Canonical Test Verification**: Test Cases 1 & 2 produce exact Decimal values (e.g., Exact: `100.00000000`, `142.85714286`, not `100.0`, `142.857`) proving strict fixed-point compliance
- **Constraint Layer**: Constitution principle "Mathematical & Execution Strictness" reinforces all monetary calculations forbidden from float
- **Status**: ✅ GREEN — Fixed-point arithmetic is mandatory and verifiable via test data; zero ambiguity

### Gate 4: BDD Acceptance Criteria ✅
- **Requirement**: All behaviors must be testable as executable Given/When/Then scenarios
- **How Satisfied**: All 4 User Stories include explicit BDD acceptance scenarios:
  - US1: 4 acceptance scenarios (Config parameter storage, defaults, type validation, edge-case values)
  - US2: 4 acceptance scenarios (exact price computation, monotonicity, scale factor, recurrence relation)
  - US3: 5 acceptance scenarios (exact amount computation, sum validation, normalization factor, multiplier scaling, dynamic capital)
  - US4: 5 acceptance scenarios (margin_type validation, multiplier constraints, order count validation)
- **Testability**: All scenarios provide Given (input parameters), When (function invoked), Then (output validated)
- **Status**: ✅ GREEN — 18 explicit BDD acceptance scenarios; all testable without ambiguity

### Gate 5: SDD Canonical Truth Reproduction ✅
- **Requirement**: Implementation MUST reproduce canonical Python bot outcomes exactly or fail critical validation
- **How Satisfied**: Feature specification Section "Canonical Test Data & Mathematical Proofs" provides three mandatory test cases with exact expected outputs derived from SDD Sections 2.0–2.2
- **Verification Mechanism**: SC-002 enforces "100% of canonical test data cases produce exact Decimal output values matching expected values to the last digit"
- **Traceability**: FR-015 requires documentation matching exactly SDD Master Report Sections 2.1, 2.2, 4.1 with section references and equation numbers
- **Status**: ✅ GREEN — Canonical test data explicit; SDD traceability required; implementation can be validated against legacy bot

**Re-evaluation After Phase 1 Design**: Constitution will be re-checked after data-model.md and contracts/ are generated to ensure entity designs and interface definitions satisfy all gates.

### Complexity Justifications

No constitution violations. Feature is foundational and must satisfy all gates before phase work proceeds.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)

```

### Source Code (Polyglot Architecture)

<!--
  MANDATORY ARCHITECTURE: This project follows a strict polyglot design with two distinct domains.
  Do NOT deviate from this structure. The core engine (Go/Rust) must remain free of orchestration,
  API, or UI concerns. Adapters go in infrastructure/. Always specify which domain a feature belongs to.
-->

```text
core-engine/             # Go/Rust - Pure Domain, Fixed-Point Math, State Machine
├── domain/              # Core trading logic, state machines, event schemas
├── infrastructure/      # Isolated Adapters (ClickHouse, Broker, message queues)
└── tests/               # Unit + integration tests proving parity with canonical Python bot

orchestrator/            # TypeScript/Python - API, Workload Distributor
├── api/                 # REST/GraphQL endpoints, request routing
├── jobs/                # Queue publishers, workload distribution
└── ui/                  # Web/CLI interfaces
```

**Feature Placement Contract**: Every feature MUST explicitly state whether it belongs to
`core-engine/` (mathematical, state, domain) or `orchestrator/` (API, jobs, UI). Core-engine
tasks MUST NOT include HTTP, API, or UI logic.
